reviews.author: Philip Blair
reviews.text: Attractive lake set within a hilly landscape. You can rent a boat to take you around. We did a relatively short circa 1.5 hour trip (cost 400k dong) including a visit to the island temple. If going again I would certainly stay for a minimum of 2 nights to give at least a day to walk part of the lake shore.

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: Ba be lake is the largest natural lake in Vietnam. Most of local people here are ethnic. Life here is still simple, not too modern, but they have essential services. I came at the end of Nov and stayed in a local homestay in Pac Ngoi village. I spent a half day for a boat tour around the lake. Experience was great! A Huge lake, water was clear, nice breeze with soft light. Body and mind relaxed completely! The landscape was beautiful, peaceful and quiet. For the boat trip, you can stop at some key attractions like: Dau Dang waterfall, Puong cave, An Ma temple, Widow island, Tien pond. Price depends on how long your trip is and how many places you want to visit, most of homestays provide boat service, or if you stayed near Ba Be national park, you can ask staffs in information center. I also took a short cycle ride through the village and visited a limestone cave nearby. A perfect place for retreat and healing.

reviews.author: Mihai
reviews.text: Ba Bể Lake is Vietnam's largest natural lake, a fantastic getaway, perfect for a two-night stay. Nestled in the mountains, it offers a peaceful retreat with stunning natural beauty. Homestays by the lake provide an authentic experience, making it an ideal weekend escape from Hanoi. Explore the lake by kayak or take a big tour boat to soak in the scenery. The surrounding caves add an adventurous touch, while winding mountain roads make the journey just as scenic. A must-visit for nature lovers seeking relaxation and adventure.

reviews.author: Yang Kevin
reviews.text: I have been here several times but summer is the most beautiful time to visit. There is a mixed colors of brown and jade at the confluence from the river when it is flowing into the lake. Stunning!

reviews.author: sonne 131
reviews.text: We enjoyed our boat trip very much. The landscape is very beautiful and the smaragd coloured water is really clean. Love it! Our guide took us to Fairy Pond and An Ma Temple. Depends on weather's condition you can swim, bamboo rafting, hiking or even biking.

reviews.author: So M
reviews.text: The largest natural lake in Vietnam, Ba Be Lake was beautiful and mesmerising. O took a boat trip to stay in one of the nearby Jungle Hotels and travelled around the lake for a few hours. There are also some small stalls which sell local produce. Highly recommend 👍👍

reviews.author: Tim Warren
reviews.text: I had a unique experience here because I was picked up on a river with my motorcycle and then taken all the way down the lake to the town. It was one of the most enjoyable things I did in Vietnam! @follow.tim.warren

reviews.author: Richie Bird
reviews.text: The lake and surrounded environment is beautiful. We used boat service which includes a tour guide who sang and gave us some highlights about the history and related information about the lake. There are two and four hours options. You can also ask them about kayak service.

reviews.author: Ken Winning
reviews.text: Would prefer to keep this place a secret to avoid it being destroyed by over-tourism and exploitation. Difficult to get around without your own transport, very limited food options. Definitely not a party place. We loved it.

reviews.author: عمرو عبد الرحمن
reviews.text: The lake itself is beautiful. However, what's around it is ridiculously annoying. Swimming isn't allowed, and the only way to enjoy the lake is through their 400K/pax tour

reviews.author: Pratik Gadgil
reviews.text: Set against the backdrop of Ba Be National Park ( NP ) ( a review is available separately), Ba Be Lake is a beautiful lake and the only means (the walkway cannot take one to the entry point ) to each of the four main attractions in the vicinity viz. Puong Cave and Dau Dang Waterfall. The green-looking lake is crystal clear and one can see the reflection of the trees surrounding it. Do note that this destination is expensive for solo travellers irrespective of whether one takes a motorbike or not, primarily because two of the four main attractions ( one cave and one waterfall ) are only accessible by boat ( and cannot be entered by walk or motorway ), and the price remains the same irrespective of passengers ( upto the boat carrying capacity ofcourse ). Currently, it is VND 1,000,000 for 1 site and a total of VND 1,500,000 for both sites. There are only two boating stations, almost opposite one another separated by this lake and it helps avoid the long main road of over 4.5 miles, however, boat tickets are expensive, and frequency is limited. There are many hotels located on the main road, which goes partially around the lake. N.B. Some of the contents from my review of the Ba Be NP may apply to this review too. N.B. Ba Be NP review had to be trimmed due to the maximum number of character limits being exceeded. N.B Some photos are common to/with Ba Be NP, and uploaded on both the pages, as deemed fit.

reviews.author: Pavla Jarkulischova
reviews.text: Absolutely beautiful area. We had the whole boat as there were no tourists for 750k (cca 4 hours), National park entrance fee is 46k, they wanted 30k for a car park. The nature, the, lake, river, cave is amazing. It was very cold on the he boat in December I recommend winter jacket.

reviews.author: Daniel Roberts
reviews.text: We did a day trip kayak through the lake. You can easily paddle up and back in one day but we did not have time for the cave or waterfall. Lots of nice homestay along the side of the lake. We preferred the area a little further along since it was more quiet

reviews.author: Sapa 1Day
reviews.text: a great place to experience culture and rest. The places to visit are not too fancy but very original. Food from restaurants is quite delicious and fresh. easily find homestays at airbnb

reviews.author: T.Nu10 Ly
reviews.text: Ba Be Lake is the largest natural lake in Viet Nam. It is a mountainous area, nearly 250 km from Ha Noi. You can get on the boat and have a trip on Ba Be Lake. During the boat trip you can visit Puong cave and the Dau Dang Waterfall. If you like to trek you can also do a trekking tour with a local guide going through the national park and villages to experiences the local communities with their daily life.

reviews.author: JEWEL TOURS Dinh Thanh
reviews.text: Beautiful place to visit, one of the 10 fresh water lake in the world, about 140m above the sea level, nice cave, Dau Dang waterfall

reviews.author: Phuong
reviews.text: 2023 clear jade water, mountains on both sides

reviews.author: Ashrinder Singh
reviews.text: For a place that claims to be a national park, it was less than impressive. There are plenty of homestays around and they come at affordable prices. After being around the northern side of Vietnam, this place just lacked the awe that some of the other cities around Vietnam had. I rented a boat which could fit 6 to 8 for 700,000 dong (per boat). The sights I visited are as follows: 1. Dong Puong Cave (this was pretty nice, a small cave the lake passes through with stalactite overhangs, and bats hidden in the cavities of the cave) 2. Lunch (we breaked for lunch at what appears to be the standard stop for all guided tours around the lake) (this came to around 140,000 dong incl. two Bia Saigon) (ask your tour guide if lunch is included) 3. Dau Dang Waterfall (there is no swimming in this falls, the water is murky, and there are piles of garbage in it) (the rapids are huge though) 4. Ferry point (this is a waterway transit point for the locals to take connecting boats, there is absolutely nothong to see here besides a land locked lake which isn't itself connected to Ba Be Lake). 5. An Ma Pagoda (this is basically a small pagoda on one of the islands, if you have been around Vietnam and seen some pagodas, this is less than likely to impress you) 6. Swim Island (At least I think thats what its called, an island with branches for you to swing yourself into the lake, though I am not sure about this if you have sensitive skin, we subsequently took a nap under the shade in the boat) The trip was then concluded. In all honesty, I am not sure what Lonely Planet was on about. They gave me the impression that Lake Ba Be is a sight to behold in Vietnam, I am sorry but it just isn't that great. The lake is littered with garbage and the sights around it are mediocre at best. All tour operators use a combination of wooden and metal chassis boats mated to a loud diesel engine that can give your inner ear a workout. They pretty much offer the same tours around the lake. Did I enjoy the boat ride? Probably, because this is likely one of the best activities around the lake. Plus, you can only see the sights if you take the boat ride. There is no way to reach them via roads or land, unless you want to trek through the jungle for hours on end. Did I enjoy the sights? They really could have been better. Would I recommend the boat tours? Sure, though go for the cheaper packages, just for the experience of being on the lake on a local boat, the sight are not all that great anyways, you wouldn't be missing much. How was my overall experience in Ba Be Lake? It was pleasant, the stars in the night sky were beautiful, the people are friendly, and it is definitely one of the most relaxing places I have been to in Vietnam. I also ate meals fit for a King so it was definitely a gastronomical experience. Disclaimer: Just my two cents.

reviews.author: Tomáš Tintěra
reviews.text: Low season is great here. Quite and genuine. Great spot to chill for two nights after doing HG (or Cao Bang) loop. Rent a kayak near "Ben Thuyen Pac Ngoi" on the south bank.

reviews.author: Allison Da Silva
reviews.text: Beautiful Lake. Lots of beautiful spots to visit If you are on a bike. Just keep riding and explore the place.

reviews.author: Leeroy Godsell
reviews.text: A beautiful lake. With some nice looking homestays around the lake, you can also take a boat trip. At this moment in time they are making a new road around the lake.

reviews.author: Oanh Do
reviews.text: I came here to film a solo camping video. How wonderful. Full video at here:

reviews.author: Huyen Do
reviews.text: Beautiful place. Very peaceful! Boat trip and the waterfall is a must go. Don't save money not taking the boat it really worth every penny. Follow the local's advice you'll have a great time.

reviews.author: KKLam
reviews.text: Beautiful serene place to relax. Comfortable weather especially during the cool winter season.

reviews.author: Ajeesh-Musthafa
reviews.text: Nice view typical tourists place. only boat ride is available. there is waterfall dau dang nearby . trekking option too

reviews.author: Seth T. Nguyen
reviews.text: The nature is great and peaceful, not much activities ... just relax and calm myself enjoy simple quitedness and absorb natural energy.

reviews.author: Brodie
reviews.text: I was underwhelmed by my experience. If your in the area anyway it may be worth your time and money but I would not go out of my way to visit.

reviews.author: Jake Mead
reviews.text: Holds the title of "the largest natural lake in Vietnam". It's very impressive and you can do day long boat tours which are good. There are also lots of homestays in the area at affordable prices.

reviews.author: Martin Cavina
reviews.text: Great place. But unfortunately on our visit it was raining

reviews.author: Nguyễn Giang Nam
reviews.text: We love the Babe national park and enjoy the boat trip, especially the Puong grotto!
